title: Distribution + closed-loop outcome (patients use it; attested help recorded)
context: This is the project's Definition of Shipped: real patients receive and use the content through the secured partner channel and it demonstrably helps them manage a treatment side effect or eat better during treatment. Success is measured as outcomes delivered to real beneficiaries, not tasks merged or pages published. All oncology-dietitian and oncologist sign-off must be recorded and content must be within its review window. Depends on nutrition-during-treatment-partner-016.
objective: Distribute the content to real patients through the secured partner channel and record that it helped them. Collect at least 10 attested instances of the content helping someone manage a side effect or eat better during treatment, via partner-mediated, consented, de-identified outcome capture. Verify safety and provenance controls in production.
acceptanceCriteria: Content distributed to real patients in active cancer treatment through the secured distribution partner channel

At least 10 attested instances recorded of the content helping a patient manage a treatment-related nutrition side effect or eat better during treatment; all outcome records are partner-mediated, fully consented, de-identified, and aggregate — no identifiable data held by this project

Safety/red-flag/banner controls verified intact and functioning in the production/distributed form; the not-medical-advice banner and escalation blocks confirmed visible and correctly rendered in the distributed format
All distributed content has recorded oncology-dietitian (RDN/RD-CSO) sign-off and oncologist sign-off where required; no content past its validUntil window served to patients
Provenance intact in all distributed forms: citations visible or accessible; source registry current; helplines verified within re-verification cadence
Outcomes and distribution reach documented in the project's governance record; project-level Definition of Shipped affirmed by the steward and oncology-dietitian reviewer
output: A governance document recording distribution to real patients, at least 10 attested (partner-mediated, de-identified) instances of the content helping patients manage treatment-related nutrition challenges, verification of safety/provenance controls in production, and affirmation of the project Definition of Shipped.
